assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies Service A as CRITICAL risk, stating it 'Will definitely block upgrades' and explains that it 'Requires ALL pods to remain available at all times' preventing node drain

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Response identifies Service B as CRITICAL risk, explicitly stating it's 'Identical to 100% availability requirement - no pods can be disrupted' and will cause 'complete upgrade blockage'

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Response categorizes Service C as LOW risk and 'Upgrade-safe', explicitly stating it 'Allows 1 pod (33%) to be unavailable during drain' and 'Node drain can evict 1 pod while 2 remain serving'

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Response categorizes Service D as LOW risk and 'Upgrade-safe', stating it 'Allows 1 pod (20%) disruption' and 'Node drain can evict 1 pod at a time'

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific fix for Service A: 'Change to minAvailable=50% or maxUnavailable=1 to allow at least one pod to be evicted' and includes YAML example with minAvailable=50%

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific fix for Service B: 'Change to maxUnavailable=1 to allow single pod eviction during drain' and includes YAML example with maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Response includes dedicated section 'GKE PDB Timeout Behavior' explaining 'GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades' and 'After 1 hour, pods are force-evicted regardless of PDB'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Response provides YAML configuration examples but does not include actual kubectl patch commands - it only shows complete YAML manifests for applying new PDB configurations

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly recommend testing in non-production environments, though it does mention testing PDBs with 'kubectl drain --dry-run' on a test node, which is not the same as testing in a non-production environment
